theme: river-delta gauge network recalibration under a fixed coordinate-update budget
title: Riverdelta Levee Network: Budgeted Gauge Recalibration
strategies: fixed-index repeat: hammer one gauge for the whole budget, leaving the rest of the network untouched (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference; trivial)
cyclic index sweep: visit gauges 0..n-1 round-robin until the budget runs out, oblivious to the coupling graph (greedy / obvious textbook coordinate descent)

block-partition + Gauss-Southwell: diagnose connected components from the edge list, give every isolated gauge exactly one visit, then repeatedly spend remaining budget on whichever coupled gauge currently has the largest exact single-step decrease g_i^2/(4*(a_i+S_i)) (strong; exploits the planted structure)
full re-optimization / multi-pass local search over update orders beyond a single greedy Southwell pass (open ceiling, uncovered)
